messages.content:
1.If you have data that comes in different formats, which characteristic of big data are you encountering? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.velocity 
b.volume 
c.variance 
d.variety
d. variety

2.Why is there a critical need for continual training and support for everyone in your organization who is exposed to big data? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.so they can better understand how to process data in the applications they use 
b.so they can better understand how to use big data to inform their own decisions 
c.so they can better understand what is being said when others talk about big data 
d.so they can better understand how big data can make decisions for them
b. so they can better understand how to use big data to inform their own decisions

3.Which type of graphics will provide the best value when you are visualizing big data? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.bar charts, histograms, and line charts 
b.the most professional-looking visualizations 
c.three-dimensional graphics 
d.interactive visualizations
d. interactive visualizations

4.How is the growth of big data best described? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.as vertical growth 
b.as circular growth 
c.as exponential growth 
d.as linear growth
c. as exponential growth

5.What is the 80/20 rule when working on a big data project? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent on wild-caught data, and 20 percent on bespoke data. 
b.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent gathering data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data. 
c.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent analyzing data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data. 
d.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent preparing data, and 20 percent is spent analyzing data.
d. This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent preparing data, and 20 percent is spent analyzing data.

6.What is the 80/20 rule when working on a big data project? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent on wild-caught data, and 20 percent on bespoke data. 
b.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent gathering data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data. 
c.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent analyzing data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data. 
d.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent preparing data, and 20 percent is spent analyzing data.
c. This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent analyzing data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data.

7.For consumers, what is usually the most important reason to bring big data out of the cloud and into the fog? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.connectivity issues 
b.low latency 
c.strengthened privacy 
d.reduced server load
b. low latency

8.What can using big data avoid in terms of interactions with both customers and potential customers? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.false positives 
b.edge cases 
c.agile responsiveness 
d.unstructured data
a. false positives

9.How can big data provide your business a competitive advantage in its operations? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.by allowing you to identify potential benefits and risks to your business 
b.by allowing you to identify new patterns and avoid any nuances in the data 
c.by allowing you to identify new trends and markets, and start engaging with them immediately 
d.by allowing you to utilize the built-in analytics for your business's websites and social media accounts
c. by allowing you to identify new trends and markets, and start engaging with them immediately

10.You would like to predict the value of future purchases by a particular client. Which method would you use via computer calculation, rather than imagining what a human would do? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.neural networks 
b.K-means clustering 
c.decision trees 
d.linear regression
d. linear regression

11.Why is it a good idea to get started in your big data journey with open data? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.Open data is diverse, plentiful, and easily accessible. 
b.Open data is small, closely held data sets. 
c.Open data does not require understanding context. 
d.Open data is company data that you have access to.
a. Open data is diverse, plentiful, and easily accessible.

12.In what area of anomaly detection will you look for outliers that have not been previously addressed? 
 
Select an answer: 
a.biometrics use 
b.potential value 
c.process failure 
d.fraud detection
b. potential value

13.Organizations already have people in place who know their own data. With this in mind, in which part of the data science Venn diagram are the most difficult skills for someone to learn?
Select an answer:     a.context
b.quant            c.code
d.substantive expertise
d. substantive expertise

13.Organizations already have people in place who know their own data. With this in mind, in which part of the data science Venn diagram are the most difficult skills for someone to learn?
Select an answer:
a.context
b.quant
c.code
d.substantive expertise
d. substantive expertise

14.Why does the minimal approach offer ineffective protection?

Select an answer:
a.because it blocks all data other than data that can be used for statistical modeling
b.because it only blocks data that is an answer to a direct question regarding personal characteristics
c.because it only blocks data that contains obvious personal identification characteristics
d.because it blocks all data containing variables correlated with personal characteristics
d. because it blocks all data containing variables correlated with personal characteristics

15.____ allow you to create big data applications and run them on basically any kind of architecture.

Select an answer:
a.Files
b.Cubes
c.Servers
d.Containers
d. Containers

16.What are the "three Vs" of big data?

Select an answer:
a.velocity, vagueness, and volume
b.volume, velocity, and variety
c.variety, validation, and vantage
d.validation, vitality, and virtue
b. volume, velocity, and variety

17.What is stream processing designed to look for?

Select an answer:
a.as much detail as desired
b.static and stationary data
c.quick trends or immediate anomalies
d.patterns, groups, and predictions
c. quick trends or immediate anomalies

What is stream processing designed to look for?

Select an answer:
a.as much detail as desired
b.static and stationary data
c.quick trends or immediate anomalies
d.patterns, groups, and predictions
c. quick trends or immediate anomalies

6.What is the 80/20 rule when working on a big data project?

Select an answer:
a.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent on wild-caught data, and 20 percent on bespoke data.
b.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent gathering data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data.
c.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent analyzing data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data.
d.This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent preparing data, and 20 percent is spent analyzing data.
c. This rule states 80 percent of your time is spent analyzing data, and 20 percent is spent preparing data.

.Why is it common in data science to break an original data set into a training data set and a testing data set?

Select an answer:
to control for loss of data in the original data set
to control for tendencies toward the role of chance
to control for unexpected market situations
to control for tendencies toward false positives
b. to control for tendencies toward the role of chance
